content: lsdir  Table of contents Prototype: lsdir(path, regex, include_base) Return type: slist Description: Returns a list of files in the directory path matching the regular expression regex . If include_base is true, full paths are returned, otherwise only names relative to the directory are returned. Arguments: path : string - Path to base directory - in the range: .+ regex : regular expression - Regular expression to match files or blank - in the range: .* include_base : - Include the base path in the list - one of true false yes no on off Example: code body common control { bundlesequence => { "example" }; } bundle agent example { vars : " listfiles " slist => lsdir ( "/etc" , "(p.sswd|gr[ou]+p)" , "true" ); " sorted_listfiles " slist => sort ( listfiles , "lex" ); reports : "files in list: $(sorted_listfiles) " ; } Output: code R: files in list: /etc/group R: files in list: /etc/passwd Tips: Filter out the current ( . ) and parent ( .. )directories with a negative look ahead. lsdir( "/tmp", "^(?!(\.$|\.\.$)).*", false ) . Notes: History: Was introduced in 3.3.0, Nova 2.2.0 (2011)
